name: Castillo de Molina de Aragón via the Camino del Cid — circular route through the Geoparque de Molina y el Alto Tajo
name_original: Castillo de Molina de Aragón por el Camino del Cid — circular por el  Geoparque de Molina y el Alto Tajo
region_intro: Hiking around Molina De Aragón offers diverse natural landscapes within the Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo Geopark and Alto Tajo Natural Park. The region is characterized by extensive river gorges and canyons, notably the Barranco de la Hoz, carved by the Tajo and Gallo rivers. Geological formations include unique "enchanted rock cities" and imposing folds. The terrain features a variety of ecosystems, including juniper, pine, oak, and holly groves, alongside open parameras and picturesque lagoons. Best hiking trails around Molina De Aragón The most popular hiking route is Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz — circular by the Parque de Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route features impressive canyons and a hill with 198 meters of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Rodenal del Corduente – circular desde Corduente – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the natural park and includes a peak with 211 meters of elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Molina de Aragón via the Camino del Cid — circular route through the Geoparque de Molina y el Alto Tajo, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through the Geopark, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Molina De Aragón is defined by river gorges, unique rock formations, and diverse forest ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 3 easy and 9 moderate routes available. The routes in Molina De Aragón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 180 hikers have used komoot to explore Molina De Aragón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in Molina De Aragón?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes in Molina De Aragón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030089334/barranco-de-la-hoz-y-virgen-de-la-hoz-circular-by-the-parque-de-molina-de-aragon-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025456681/castillo-de-molina-de-aragon-via-the-camino-del-cid-circular-route-through-the-geoparque-de-molina-y-el-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Molina de Aragón via the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on a hike in Molina De Aragón?
A: Molina De Aragón, part of the Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo Geopark, is known for its dramatic river gorges and canyons, especially those carved by the Tajo and Gallo rivers. You'll also encounter unique geological formations like "enchanted rock cities," diverse ecosystems including juniper and pine groves, and picturesque lagoons and waterfalls such as Escaleruela and Poveda.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Molina De Aragón?
A: Yes, the region offers trails for various fitness levels. There are 3 easy routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These paths allow you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant physical challenge.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I visit while hiking in the area?
A: Many hiking routes integrate historical sites. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;, the Tower of Aragón, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Hoz&lt;/a&gt;. The long-distance &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025456681/castillo-de-molina-de-aragon-via-the-camino-del-cid-circular-route-through-the-geoparque-de-molina-y-el-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; also passes through the region, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Molina De Aragón?
A: The routes in Molina De Aragón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-preserved natural landscapes, the impressive river gorges, and the variety of trails suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots worth visiting?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts numerous viewpoints offering stunning vistas, particularly over the Barranco de la Hoz. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4322842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Hoces del Río Piedra from Mirador del Reconquillo&lt;/a&gt; are highly recommended for their panoramic views of the dramatic canyons.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by caves or unique rock formations?
A: Yes, the geological diversity of Molina De Aragón is a highlight. You can explore fascinating rock cities like those in Chequilla and Peñalén, and visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6385115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Way of the Cross&lt;/a&gt;, which is accessible via routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barranco de la Hoz – Cave of the Way of the Cross loop from Corduente&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Molina De Aragón?
A: The diverse ecosystems and landscapes of Molina De Aragón make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be warm but ideal for exploring river areas. Winter hikes are also possible, especially in areas with less snow, offering a different, serene experience.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or lead to water features?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park is defined by its extensive river systems. Many trails follow the Tajo and Gallo rivers, leading through impressive gorges and past features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6621844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallo River Ford&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find picturesque lagoons and waterfalls throughout the region.

Q: How long do hiking trails in Molina De Aragón typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Moderate trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1121724970/el-rodenal-del-corduente-circular-desde-corduente-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rodenal del Corduente&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 3 to 3.5 hours for a 7-8 mile (12 km) distance. Shorter, easier routes will naturally take less time.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The natural park is rich in biodiversity. You might spot carnivorous mammals like badgers and wild cats, or ungulates such as roe deer and deer. Birdwatchers can look out for griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, peregrine falcons, and various forest and steppe birds.
tips: In the castle of Molina it is necessary to distinguish the fortress surrounded by walls, and the so-called Torre de Aragón. The fortress is from the 12th century and was built by Manrique de Lara, the first lord of Molina. The Torre de Aragón was built on the Arab castle, and this on a previous Celtiberian castro.
Spanish municipality in the province of Guadalajara through which the tourist route called Camino del Cid passes.
